researchNotes: Most individual creature wiki pages are marked as stubs. Numerical stats sourced from /Data subpages where available. Resistances/vulnerabilities to specific damage types are not documented on the wiki; the game uses 6 damage types (Kinetic, Fire, Electrical, Acid, Cold, Plasma) but per-enemy resistances are not published. HP scaling scalars are per-creature and vary; only Haz1 and Haz5 endpoints are documented on wiki data pages. Biome HP/damage modifiers are additive percentage increases on top of base stats.

damageTypes.name: Kinetic
damageTypes.effect: Basic non-elemental damage. No status effect.

damageTypes.name: Fire
damageTypes.effect: Inflicts Burn — deals heavy damage over time.

damageTypes.name: Electrical
damageTypes.effect: Inflicts Shock — increases critical hit chance against the target.

damageTypes.name: Acid
damageTypes.effect: Inflicts Corrode — increases all damage taken from all sources.

damageTypes.name: Cold
damageTypes.effect: Inflicts Slow — decreases move speed; stacking chance to Freeze.

damageTypes.name: Plasma
damageTypes.effect: Shots bounce off targets and terrain.

enemies.name: Brown Grunt

hazardScaling.notes: Scalar applies to damage output. HP also scales; exact per-level values not documented.

enemies.notes: The most basic hostile unit. Walks directly toward the player and bites. Forms the bulk of every swarm. Tier 1 (brown) is the baseline; Tier 2 (blue) and Tier 3 (reddish) variants have increased HP but otherwise identical behavior.

enemies.name: Blue Grunt
enemies.hpNotes: Higher HP than Brown Grunt; exact value not documented on wiki
enemies.notes: Identical behavior to Brown Grunt but with notably more health. Appears in later stages and higher hazard levels.

enemies.name: Red Grunt
enemies.hpNotes: Highest HP of the grunt tiers; exact value not documented on wiki
enemies.notes: Reddish-brown coloration. Identical behavior to Brown Grunt but tankiest of the three grunt tiers.

enemies.name: Fast Grunt
enemies.notes: The fastest basic hostile unit. Considerably faster than regular grunts but with less health. Charges straight at the player. Small body size (0.6 units diameter) makes it harder to hit.

enemies.name: Slasher
enemies.notes: Upgraded melee enemy. More health and higher damage than the basic Grunts. Larger size (1 unit diameter). A significant step up from basic grunts — high priority threat in dense swarms.

enemies.name: Acid Spitter
enemies.notes: Keeps its distance from the player and spits purple acid projectiles. Will not close to melee range — actively retreats when the player approaches. Elite variant shoots a spread of five shots. Must be prioritized or kited.

enemies.name: Exploder

hazardScaling.notes: Does not scale with hazard level

enemies.notes: Orange Glyphid that approaches the player and detonates in a large AOE. Can be killed at range before it reaches the player to trigger a safe explosion. Explosion can also kill nearby bugs. Killing from range to chain-explode enemy clusters is a valid strategy. Does not scale with hazard level.

enemies.name: Mini Exploder
enemies.hpNotes: Smaller/weaker version of Exploder; exact value not documented
enemies.notes: Purple variant of the Exploder, exclusive to Azure Weald. Much faster and more numerous than regular Exploders. The speed and quantity make them more dangerous than their standard counterpart.

enemies.name: Praetorian

hazardScaling.notes: Listed as not scaling with hazard in standard sense, but has haz5Scalar of 1.3

enemies.notes: Large, heavily armored Glyphid. High knockback resistance. Can slowly dig through terrain to pursue the player — walls offer no escape. Heavy priority target that soaks enormous damage. Elite Praetorians can break terrain much more effectively.

enemies.name: Big Praetorian
enemies.hpNotes: Higher HP than standard Praetorian; exact value not documented
enemies.notes: Blue, oversized Praetorian variant, exclusive to Azure Weald. Identical behavior to regular Praetorian but with significantly more HP. Treat as a priority threat.

enemies.name: Mactera
enemies.wikiName: Mactera / Flying Seeker / Dark Green Mactera
enemies.notes: Flying melee enemies that can pass over all terrain — walls, vines, and voids are irrelevant. Appear in groups. In Hollow Bough they fly in clusters. Basic Mactera fly idly and only deal contact damage; they do not actively pursue the player. Elite Mactera actively chase the player.

enemies.name: Q'ronar Youngling

hazardScaling.notes: Listed as not scaling with hazard in standard sense, but has haz5Scalar of 1.3

enemies.notes: Exclusive to Salt Pits. Extremely fast movement (5.4 speed — fastest basic unit). Travels aimlessly, bouncing off walls. Complete immunity to freeze. The Elite Q'ronar Shellback variant stops near the player to fire a beam of acid, dealing considerable damage.

enemies.name: Stabber Vine
enemies.hpNotes: Exact HP not documented on Survivor wiki
enemies.notes: Stationary melee enemy — attacks the player only when they approach within range. Does not move. Most common in Hollow Bough but can appear elsewhere. Easily avoided by maintaining awareness of placement.

enemies.name: Spitball Infector
enemies.hpNotes: Exact HP not documented on Survivor wiki
enemies.notes: Stationary ranged enemy that fires large green projectile shots. Does not move. Must be destroyed or avoided — their projectiles can interrupt positioning and deal consistent damage.

enemies.name: Warden
enemies.hpNotes: Exact HP not documented on wiki
enemies.notes: Unique champion exclusive to Azure Weald. Does not attack directly. Periodically stops moving to create a mind link — a purple aura that grants all nearby enemies considerable damage reduction until the Warden is killed or the player stands inside the aura radius. Multiple Wardens stack their auras, making clustered groups extremely dangerous. Strategy: Enter the aura radius to temporarily disable it, then kill the Warden. If multiple stack, lead other enemies away first.

enemies.name: Grunt Mini Elite
enemies.hpNotes: Much stronger than Brown Grunt; exact value not documented
enemies.notes: Purple-colored mini elite variant of the Grunt. Deals and takes more damage than standard grunts. Considerably faster than regular grunts. Spawns in groups mid-swarm.

enemies.name: Fast Grunt Mini Elite
enemies.notes: Purple mini elite variant of the Fast Grunt. Even faster than the already-speedy basic Fast Grunt. High threat due to movement speed.

enemies.name: Slasher Mini Elite
enemies.notes: Purple mini elite variant of the Slasher. Deals and takes more damage than standard Slashers, and moves faster. Priority target.

enemies.name: Spitter Mini Elite
enemies.notes: Purple mini elite variant of the Acid Spitter. Fires a spread of three projectiles instead of one. Retreats from the player. Significantly more dangerous than the standard Spitter due to the spread shot.

enemies.name: Mactera Mini Elite
enemies.hpNotes: Less health than Elite Mactera but more than standard Mactera
enemies.notes: Purple mini elite flying variant. Unlike regular Mactera that fly idly, Mini Elite Mactera actively pursue the player. Behaves like a smaller Elite Mactera.

enemies.name: Q'ronar Youngling Mini Elite
enemies.notes: Mini elite variant of the Q'ronar Youngling. Exclusive to Salt Pits. Combines the extreme speed of Q'ronar Younglings with enhanced mini-elite stats.

enemies.name: Elite Slasher
enemies.hpNotes: Elite tier — significantly higher HP than standard Slasher; exact value not documented
enemies.notes: Stage boss for stages 1-4. Has significantly increased health, speed, and damage compared to standard Slashers. Can break terrain much more effectively than even regular Praetorians. Defeating quickly (within the Alien Threat Level timer) grants better loot crate quality.

enemies.name: Elite Praetorian
enemies.hpNotes: Elite tier — significantly higher HP than standard Praetorian; exact value not documented
enemies.notes: Stage boss for stages 1-4. Extremely durable. Can break terrain much more effectively than standard Praetorians. Combined with high health makes it a prolonged, dangerous encounter.

enemies.name: Elite Acid Spitter
enemies.hpNotes: Elite tier — significantly higher HP than standard Acid Spitter
enemies.notes: Stage boss for stages 1-4. Fires five projectiles in a spread pattern instead of one. Keeps distance and retreats from melee. The spread shot dramatically increases its zone-denial capability.

enemies.name: Elite Mactera
enemies.notes: Stage boss for stages 1-4. Actively and relentlessly pursues the player, unlike standard Mactera that fly idly. High resistances to crowd control (freeze, slow, knockback all at 50%). Must be out-damaged rather than kited.

enemies.name: Elite Q'ronar Shellback
enemies.hpNotes: Elite tier — high HP; exact value not documented
enemies.notes: Stage boss, Salt Pits exclusive. Behaves like a Q'ronar Youngling normally (very fast, bouncing movement) but when near the player, stops to fire a sustained beam of acid that deals considerable damage. The transition from rapid movement to stationary acid beam makes it unpredictable.

enemies.name: Lootbug
enemies.notes: Fully passive creature. Minds its own business and will not attack. Can be killed for resources/gold. Flees when threatened (flee distance: 10 units, trigger: 8 units). Not directly targetable in Survivor mode.

enemies.name: Golden Lootbug
enemies.notes: Larger, shinier variant of the Lootbug. Drops significantly more gold/resources. Worth hunting down whenever encountered. Appears in groups of up to 3 (pool size: 3). Spawns rarely.

enemies.name: Huuli Hoarder
enemies.notes: Uncommon passive creature. Quickly runs away when attacked. Drops valuable resources if killed before it escapes. High-value target — worth diverting from combat briefly if spotted.

bosses.name: Glyphid Dreadnought

phases.description: Three smaller cocoons must be destroyed first to draw the Dreadnought out of its main cocoon.
Fully active boss. Performs long-distance leaps dealing massive damage. Ground smashes create a funnel of rock pillars. Fires swarm eggs that spawn additional bugs.

bosses.mechanics: Two-phase encounter. Stage 5 only. Player must locate and destroy 3 satellite cocoons to force the Dreadnought out of its main cocoon. Full knockback immunity. High slow/freeze resistance. Highest single-target DPS threat in the game.
bosses.notes: One of two final boss encounters. The non-twin variant. Towers over all other enemies. Leaping attack covers large distances — keep moving. Egg spawns add ongoing swarm pressure. Defeating it drops Gear.

bosses.name: Glyphid Dreadnought Twins

phases.description: Both Lacerator and Arbalest are active simultaneously. Health bars are linked — if they diverge too far, they heal each other to equalize HP.

bosses.mechanics: Dual boss encounter. The Lacerator (purple, melee) charges and body-slams the player, also performing the same long-distance leap attack as the Regular Dreadnought. The Arbalest (orange, ranged) maintains distance and fires a spread of fireballs. Crucially, if one twin's HP drops too far ahead of the other, they heal each other to equalize — players must whittle both down together. At higher hazard levels both move faster, deal more damage, and use specials more frequently.

lacerator.description: Purple, melee-focused twin. Deals melee contact damage. Shares the Dreadnought's leaping attack.

arbalest.description: Orange, ranged-focused twin. Keeps distance. Fires a spread of fireballs.

bosses.notes: The alternate final boss. Harder than the Regular Dreadnought due to managing two targets simultaneously. Kill both at similar health rates — the linked healing mechanic punishes focusing one target exclusively. Prioritize the Arbalest if you need breathing room, but balance damage across both.
